projects.title: The Orchestra Chuck Built
projects.logline: A former attorney turned conductor builds the nation's largest majority Black and Latino youth orchestra.
projects.description: A documentary portrait of Chuck Dickerson, who left the law to lead the nation's largest majority Black and Latino youth orchestra — confronting the fact that Black musicians make up just 2.3% of American orchestras. A story about mentorship, music, and who gets to belong on the concert stage.

affiliations.label: Producers
affiliations.value:
Ryan Suffern, Maryann Garger, Christopher Stoudt,
Bob Logan, Ains "Brainz" Prasad

projects.title: Four Seasons Total Documentary
projects.logline: The improbable true story behind the press conference that became a defining image of the 2020 election.
projects.description: An examination of the November 7, 2020 press conference at Four Seasons Total Landscaping — held between a crematorium and an adult bookstore — as the presidential election was called. A film about spin, chance, and the surreal machinery of American politics.

affiliations.label: Production Companies
affiliations.value: Sutter Road Picture Company, Zipper Bros Films, Chill Factory

affiliations.label: Producers
affiliations.value: Christopher Stoudt, Glen Zipper, Sean Stuart, Chris Paonessa, Doc Crotzer, Kevin Middleton

projects.title: Adaptive: Paris (S01)
projects.logline: Adaptive follows four world-class athletes on their relentless pursuit of the podium at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games.
projects.description: Off the field, they’re powerful advocates for inclusion and equity in the disability community. And as the spotlight turns to Paris, they’ll put everything on the line—not just for medals, but to show the world what it truly means to be elite.

affiliations.label: Production Companies
affiliations.value:
Vitium Productions, Far Between Pictures,
Cookie Jar & A Dream Studios, TFA Group

affiliations.label: Producers
affiliations.value:
Christopher Stoudt, Clayton Frech, Jay Snyder,
Colin Weatherby, Ryan Suffern

affiliations.label: Executive Producers
affiliations.value: Dave Pantano, Ezra Frech, Bahar Soomekh, Bill Bindley, Mike Karz, Jay Ruderman, Mallory Weggemann

projects.title: Camp ALEC
projects.logline: At a summer camp in Michigan, non-speaking kids find their voices through communication technology.
projects.description: A literacy-focused summer camp where non-speaking children learn to communicate using electronic AAC devices. Co-produced with The Kennedy/Marshall Company and released as part of the Disney+ 'People & Places' series.

projects.title: One Buck Won't Hurt
projects.logline: Four Black teenagers tap dance for tips in the French Quarter — from adolescence to adulthood.
projects.description: A coming-of-age documentary following four Black teenagers who tap dance for tips in New Orleans' French Quarter, tracing their lives from adolescence to adulthood against the backdrop of youth and incarceration.
recognition: Alameda Film Festival — Best Documentary Short
BendFilm — Special Jury Award for Social Impact

affiliations.label: Producers
affiliations.value: Christopher Stoudt, Jed Rapp Goldstein, Ryan Suffern

projects.title: Lost LA: Descanso Gardens
projects.logline: One beloved public garden becomes a lens on the social and political history of Los Angeles.
projects.description: Tracing Southern California's beloved public garden from pre-colonial oak woodland to its contemporary role as a museum, examining the social and political evolution of Los Angeles along the way.
recognition: Los Angeles Emmy nomination — Outstanding Director

affiliations.label: Producers
affiliations.value: Matthew Crotty, Nathan Masters, Austin Simons

projects.title: Daddy Business
projects.logline: A single father confronts his own trauma to break the cycle for his son.
projects.description: A portrait of single father Christopher 'Tattoo' Fontes as he confronts his own trauma to break generational cycles for his son, Damian.

projects.title: Sonic — Live Free Eat Sonic
projects.logline: Sonic celebrates America's 250th birthday.
projects.description: Live free. Eat Sonic.

affiliations.label: Editors
affiliations.value: Christopher Stoudt, Scott Shepherd, Adrian Randall

projects.title: Beyond the Vest
projects.logline: Beyond the Vest follows the employees of a Sam's Club in Columbus, Georgia, revealing the lives, dreams, and unexpected stories behind the blue vests.

projects.title:
The Office -
Credits Parody
projects.logline: Shot-for-shot remake of The Office opening credits — Four Seasons Total Landscaping edition.

projects.title: Goat Milk Soap
projects.logline: Great skin care doesn't start in a lab. It starts in a barn. Meet the goats — and the people — behind Chivas Skin Care.
projects.description: Produced for Vid.me as a pilot for their “Maker” web series.

projects.title: Patron Saint of Jazz Fest
projects.logline: For the die-hards, the New Orleans Jazz & Heritage Festival is closer to religion.
projects.description: A celebration of the New Orleans Jazz & Heritage Festival told through three devoted attendees whose participation borders on religious devotion.

projects.title: Innocence Project New Orleans
projects.logline: After 46 years of wrongful incarceration, Wilbert Jones walks free.
projects.description: Documenting the release of Wilbert Jones after 46 years of wrongful incarceration, and the work of Innocence Project New Orleans, which has freed more than 30 innocent prisoners across Louisiana and Mississippi.

projects.title: Skater Girl
projects.logline: Eight-year-old Sky Brown came to America from Japan with her parents and younger brother, Ocean, to skateboard…and she's making plenty of waves in the process.
projects.description: Just this year, Sky became the youngest girl to compete against adults at the Vans' Open Pro Series. But shaking up the male-dominated sport is hardly a big deal. For Sky, if she can inspire future generations of girls to pursue their dreams, she'd be as happy.

projects.title: Silicone Dolls
projects.logline: Matt and his team of doll makers have perfected the human body.
projects.description: In look and size, their silicone human replicas are as close to the real thing as you can get without a pulse. The future is here and it's straight out of science fiction.

projects.title: Laughter Yoga
projects.logline: Jeffrey Briar trains members of the Laughter Yoga Institute, a laughing club in Laguna Beach, California that works on the principle that the body cannot differentiate between “real” and deliberate laughter.
projects.description: Science suggests that laughter of both kinds can promote health benefits like cardiovascular fitness and better mood. Most importantly, the laughter yoga class can help total strangers forge new friendships — all to a lively laugh track.

projects.title: Gator Wrestlers
projects.logline: With no structured competitions and a ludicrously high death potential, gator wrestling might be the most dangerous and pointless sport there is, but it's still got a strong following among Southerners like T. Michael Kliebert, who grew up in the swamps.
projects.description: For them, it's all about tradition, and Mr. Kliebert explains why.

projects.title: Tiny Foods
projects.logline: Performance artist and unlikely chef Tom Brown is bringing strangers together around the tiny kitchen, where he's serving up tiny foods and words of wisdom.
projects.description: Along with his fully functional portable kitchen, Tom has made more than 300 utensils and tools that he uses to cook up real, edible miniature foods. He may be passing out free lunches, but the gifts he gets from his customers are worth all the work.

projects.title: Mr. Okra
projects.logline: In the vibrant city of New Orleans, Mr. Okra is a man like no other.
projects.description: For the last 50 years, Arthur Robinson, aka Mr. Okra, has been delivering fresh produce in the back of his pickup truck to his community. It's enough to make him quite the celebrity in his little pocket of the world, and one of the last singing vegetable vendors.

projects.title: Water Sommelier
projects.logline: Los Angeles's first and only "water sommelier," Martin Riese of Patina Restaurant, is working to educate people about the world's most popular drink.
projects.description: In his Water 101 class, Riese offers patrons the chance to join him for a water tasting — for $50 a head.

projects.title: Obama Impersonator
projects.logline: Reggie Brown never thought he'd be known for his looks, but that all changed around the time Barack Obama was hitting the national stage.
projects.description: With his uncanny resemblance to the 44th President of the United States, Reggie has become the most famous Barack Obama impersonator in the world. And with the new President Donald Trump's administration settling in, Reggie's star continues to shine.

projects.title: The People Walker
projects.logline: Chuck McCarthy of Los Angeles, California, is a professional people walker.
projects.description: For $7 a mile, The People Walker will escort you wherever you need to go, acting as a chaperone/chauffeur hybrid who doubles as a therapist, a friend and whatever else you seek in a sidewalk stroll. What's more, Chuck, who's also an actor, benefits too: he's lost weight, made new friends, and found new perspectives while running — that is, walking — his own business.

projects.title: Weed Nuns
projects.logline: The Sisters of the Valley is a medicinal cannabis company run by Christine Meeusen and Darcy Johnson in California's Central Valley.
projects.description: Their mission? To erase the negative stigma around weed, and end human suffering as best they can. Their iconic nun outfits aren't worn as part of any organized religion — but rather as a sign of devotion to the plant.

projects.title: The Grandma Gamer
projects.logline: 80-year-old Shirley Curry defies grandma stereotypes by becoming one of the oldest avid Skyrim players in the world.
projects.description: She believes video games keep her mind active and says other people her age could benefit greatly from slaying virtual dragons.

projects.title: Superman of Hollywood Boulevard
projects.logline: 48-year-old Christopher Dennis' life didn't go as planned.
projects.description: After some early success as an actor and model, Dennis struggled with drugs and eventually found himself on Hollywood Boulevard, working for tips from tourists as a Superman impersonator. Still, Dennis finds purpose in brightening other peoples' lives.

projects.title: I'm a Furry
projects.logline: 19-year-old Christopher from California overcomes his social anxiety by dressing up as a neon green fox named Kona.
projects.description: But it's not just about making himself feel better, it's about spreading the happiness as far as it will go.

projects.title: Slow to Rise: Katrina, Ten Years Later
projects.logline: Ten years after Katrina, a New Orleans neighborhood is still waiting to rise.
projects.description: Featured on Huffington Post.

projects.title: The Homeless Jedi
projects.logline: 32-year-old Ace Venom (his legal name) identifies as a Jedi realist.
projects.description: And like Jedi from Star Wars films, Ace relies upon a philosophy of righteousness and helping others to endure his day-to-day struggles in Portland, where rising rent prices have left him and his fiancée homeless.

projects.title: New Orleans Boxing Club
projects.logline: A short film produced in collaboration with the New Orleans lifestyle brand DNO and the New Orleans Boxing Club.

projects.title: Ryan's Recycling
projects.logline: When Ryan Hickman was just 3 years old, he went with his father to recycle for the first time.
projects.description: After sorting his bottles and cans, he became obsessed with recycling, and how it can help save the environment, and decided to start his own business – Ryan’s Recycling Company. Now 7, Ryan has disposed of more than 200,000 bottles and cans and earned about $21,000. It’s all in a hard day’s work.

projects.title: Celebrity Autograph Hunter
projects.logline: 37-year-old Michael Medlin, known around Hollywood as “Red Bull,” has turned autograph collecting into a bustling business.
projects.description: It’s hard work and it gets messy, but for this movie fanatic, getting up close and personal with Hollywood’s elite is enough to stomach the struggle.

projects.title: Urban Beekeeping
projects.logline: Some people have dogs, George Langworthy has bees.
projects.description: In Los Angeles, where it is now legal to keep hives in your backyard, George is one of a number of beekeepers helping to preserve bee species that have seen a severe drop in numbers over the last several years. And his work could not be any more important.

projects.title: Treeman of Venice
projects.logline: A self-described artist and teacher, 57-year-old Lionel Powell dresses up as a tree every day and roams the Venice Beach boardwalk, spreading his “smell the roses” philosophy as the Treeman of Venice.
projects.description: Want to know the answers to life’s biggest questions? Ask a tree. This is what keeps him going in a world that values money over happiness.

projects.title: The Black Rebel
projects.logline: 22-year-old Andrew Duncomb has become one of the most prominent Confederate flag supporters in the nation — largely because of the color of his skin.
projects.description: A black man raised by white parents, Duncomb believes the controversial symbol represents heritage, not hate, but the majority of America disagrees. And he doesn't care one bit. This is his story.

projects.title: Building a Healthy Neighborhood
projects.logline: St. Roch is a historic New Orleans neighborhood nestled between the famous Marigny, Bywater and 7th Ward neighborhoods.
projects.description: With more than 65% of the residents in this community considered low-income earning less than $15,000 a year; 16% of the neighbors are 65 years or older. St. Roch is a prime neighborhood for reinvestment targeting older adults. Many homeowners have lived in the community for generations and most have experienced the destruction Hurricane Katrina brought to their neighborhood.

projects.title: Transgender Boxer
projects.logline: Before his sexual reassignment surgery, Pat Manuel was a highly decorated female boxer and a women’s boxing Olympic hopeful.
projects.description: Now, following his transition, Manuel competes in the men’s division — and makes waves as an outspoken advocate for transgender equality.

projects.title: Adult Thumbsucker
projects.logline: As an adult thumbsucker, Harvey Miller feels like his favorite habit is largely misunderstood.
projects.description: For him and others like him, thumbsucking is a harmless way to fill a void. And who can argue with that?

projects.title: BBall Girl
projects.logline: At just six years old, Jaliyah Manuel is already catching the attention of basketball pros.
projects.description: But while she dreams of one day becoming a superstar in the WNBA, her father is focused on making sure she has the best future ahead of her.

projects.title: Real-life Fangsmith
projects.logline: A former dental technician who now goes by the name Father Sebastiaan travels the world fitting real-life vampires with custom fangs.
projects.description: In this video, he explains how his unusual occupation gives his life purpose.

projects.title: I Have a Stutter
projects.logline: Due to a severe stutter, Ray Demnitz often struggles to say his name.
projects.description: He struggles to place orders at restaurants. Finishing any sentence can prove challenging. Even though he may struggle to get the words out, he’s a great communicator. Rather than let his stutter hold him back, Ray has proudly embraced it as a mark of his individuality — and others might do well to learn from his example.

projects.title: I Have Alopecia
projects.logline: “I have alopecia,” says Kylie Bamberger, who started losing her hair at age 12.
projects.description: By the time she turned 15, she was completely bald. In the time since, she’s moved past her struggles with alopecia to embrace life and help kids struggling with the hair loss disease to do the same.

projects.title: The Football Giant
projects.logline: 7-foot 440-pound high school senior John Krahn, 17, just might be the largest football player ever.
projects.description: But even though he towers over his competition, Krahn’s life aspirations aren’t all football - so what does someone his size dream about becoming if the NFL doesn’t come knocking?

projects.title: Santa Dave
projects.logline: 61-year-old Dave Morrison has been making children smile for the last decade as a Santa-for-hire.
projects.description: But every holiday season, Dave is reminded of his own children who he hasn’t seen in years.

projects.title: Art of the Paparazzi
projects.logline: 32-year-old Yori Uehara has worked as a paparazzo in L.A. for nearly a decade.
projects.description: He's learned that being a successful “pap” takes quick wits, a sharp eye and years of experience. He also learned that photographing celebrities doesn’t automatically make you a terrible person.

projects.title: The Zen of Sign Spinning
projects.logline: 20-year-old Oshay Henderson hits the sweltering streets of L.A. every day to spin signs for a living.
projects.description: According to him, it’s the best job in the world. But he’s a lot more than just some guy on the side of road - he may just have life figured out.

projects.title: Samurai in the Modern World
projects.logline: Toshishiro Obata is a master of Japanese swordsmanship who comes from a long line of samurai, dating back to the 15th century.
projects.description: As a samurai living in the 21st century, Toshishiro has a unique opinion about technology, tradition, and the importance of being independent.

projects.title: What 'Straight Outta Compton' Means in Compton
projects.logline: As reports circulated that police would be increasing security in Compton to handle the threat of gang violence on opening night of the N.W.A. film Straight Outta Compton, locals stepped out in peace to show heartfelt support for their hometown rap group.

projects.title: Professional Dog Dancer
projects.logline: Patie Ventre, lifelong animal lover and dog dancing champion has only one thing on her mind — the Olympics.
projects.description: For her, it's just a matter of time before this unusual sport is accepted by the world.

projects.title: Grandma Bodybuilder
projects.logline: 68-year-old Ruby Pikes began entering bodybuilding competitions at age 51 and has since dedicated her life to fitness and helping other older women reach their fitness goals.
projects.description: Here she explains what keeps her going and why age is no excuse to stay unhealthy.

projects.title: The Satanic Temple
projects.logline: The Satanic Temple of Los Angeles, co-founded by 50-year-old William Morrison, was established not as a religious order but as a political movement whose primary mission is to defend first amendment rights against the encroachment of the American Christian agenda.
projects.description: Here, Morrison explains why the group has marked the day 6/6/16 with a visit to City Hall in Lancaster, CA.

projects.title: California's Teen Smokers
projects.logline: California's new law raising the age at which one can legally purchase cigarettes to 21 went into effect on June 9, 2016.
projects.description: Teen smokers around the state are now trying to kick the habit — or looking for more unsavory ways to feed their habit. Here are three youngsters caught in the throes of this struggle.

projects.title: LA Street Cobbler
projects.logline: Rafael Lopez doesn’t have much, but what he has he shares.
projects.description: Since coming to America 30 years ago, Rafael has been making what he can as a cobbler on the streets of Echo Park, Los Angeles. Someday, he hopes, all of his hard work and shoe repairs will pay off.

projects.title: Speedcubers
projects.logline: Welcome to the world of speedcubing, the fast-paced puzzle competition that is growing in popularity around the world – even if you’ve never heard of it.
projects.description: Cubers like Michael Young, from West Hollywood, are battling to see who can solve the Rubik’s cube in the fastest time.

projects.title: Black Santa Claus
projects.logline: Santa is about peace, love and putting a smile on children’s faces.
projects.description: And for the children in this Southern California neighborhood, Santa is looking a lot more like them. Meet Langston Patterson, the new face of the holidays. As the black Santa Claus at the Baldwin Hills Crenshaw Plaza in Los Angeles, Patterson is hoping to bring children of all ages and races together to celebrate the holidays in a loving way. And judging by the looks on his fans’ faces, it’s working.

projects.title: Dad in Jail
projects.logline: With their dad in jail, twin teens Justice and Freedom have been forced to make a lot of sacrifices for their family.
projects.description: Before they were born, the twins’ father was convicted of a crime and sent to jail. But despite his time in prison, their father has been a constant in their lives. It is because of this unique bond that the family has grown stronger together – incarceration and all.

projects.title: The Chainsaw Artist
projects.logline: There’s something dangerous about Stacy Poitras’ sculptures.
projects.description: One mistake while carving could result in a ruined chainsaw carving...or even worse. But that’s to be expected when he’s using a chainsaw as a paintbrush. After dropping out of art school to pursue chainsaw art as part of CMT’s Chainsaw Gang, Stacy has gained a new perspective on life and what it means to be an artist.

projects.title: Stunt Driver
projects.logline: 20-year old Brionna Lynch has always had a passion for cars and driving fast.
projects.description: Since the moment she turned 16 and got her license, she’s been tearing up the streets on and off screen. And now, as one of the only females in the stunt driving profession, she’s carving a path in movies such as The Fate of the Furious and Ninja Turtles 2.

projects.title: Food Truck for the Homeless
projects.logline: After a life-changing epiphany in 2009, Ravi Singh, along with his wife Jacquelin, started Share a Meal, a non-profit program that delivers fresh meals to the homeless on Los Angeles’ Skid Row.
projects.description: For five days a week, the Sikh man and his team of volunteers make and deliver hundreds of vegan burritos out of their food truck as a way to serve and share with the community. Out here, it’s more than just a free meal – it’s a way to show others that they have not been forgotten.
